kept.text:
Premarket movers:

OpenAI partners such as CoreWeave (CRWV -7%) and Oracle (ORCL -7%) tumble after the WSJ reported that the AI startup recently failed to meet targets for sales and new users, reviving worries about spending ahead of tech earnings. Stocks linked to the buildout of AI infrastructure — from computing providers to the makers of semiconductors and power equipment used in data centers — are also down after the Wall Street Journal report on OpenAI.

Magnificent Seven stocks are also mostly lower: Nvidia falls 2% on the OpenAI report (Apple +0.4%, Alphabet -0.1%, Amazon -0.9%, Meta Platforms -0.8%, Microsoft -1.2%, Tesla -1.2%)

Celestica (CLS) falls 13% after the maker of electronic components reported first-quarter results that featured smaller upside to expectations than in recent quarters. While it raised its full-year forecast, analysts said the company had been facing high expectations.

Dynatrace (DT) gains 4% on a report that Starboard Value LP took a stake and is pushing the company to better capitalize on the shift to artificial intelligence.

Erasca (ERAS) slides 40% after the biotech said one patient withdrew from the trial after a severe treatment-related adverse event and later died, according to a filing.

General Motors (GM) rises 4% after raising its profit outlook for the year by $500 million, saying its pickups and sport utility vehicles continue to sell even as gasoline prices soar due to the war in Iran.

LendingClub (LC) rises 9% after the online lender’s first-quarter revenue and net interest income beat the average analyst estimate.

Nucor (NUE) rises 2% after the steelmaker reported first-quarter earnings per share that beat the average analyst estimate as steel shipments were stronger than expected.

Rambus (RMBS) plunges 17% after the semiconductor device manufacturer reported first-quarter results that were largely in line with expectations, which analysts said was a disappointment in the wake of recent strength in the stock.

Sanmina (SANM) rises 7% after the electronics contract manufacturing services company’s second-quarter results beat expectations and it gave a full-year outlook that is seen as positive.

Solaris Energy (SEI) rallies 5% after the firm’s first-quarter Ebitda beat the average analyst estimate.

Spotify Technology falls 11% after reporting results that underwhelmed Wall Street, forecasting operating income in the current quarter that missed analysts’ estimates.

UPS (UPS) falls 3% after the courier left financial guidance unchanged. Its profit beat expectations in the first quarter.

kept.text:
Hats off to the $GLXY Data Centers team on delivering the first data hall to CoreWeave!

In a market where nearly half of U.S. data center projects slated for 2026 are reported to be delayed or canceled, execution is what separates the teams that actually deliver from the rest.

Yes, power is scarce, but power doesn’t build data centers. It takes deep in-house expertise across design, engineering, operations, power, procurement and capital markets. It takes strong partnerships with tier one contractors. It takes 1,000+ people, unrelenting coordination and countless hours working toward a single outcome.

No large-scale build goes perfectly to plan. Weather, supply chain disruptions, and infrastructure challenges are part of the process. That’s exactly what makes this milestone so meaningful.

Many more to come.

kept.text:
WSJ報道によれば、OpenAIは直近でChatGPTの新規ユーザー数と売上の社内目標を達成できなかったとされる。

これを受けて、SoftBank、Oracle、CoreWeave、Arm、AI半導体関連に売りが波及した。

ただ、ここで少し違和感がある。

市場は「ChatGPTの一般ユーザー成長が鈍化した」と見てAI関連を売った。
でも、2026年のAIプラットフォーム戦争の本丸は、もはや一般向けチャットボットの週間アクティブユーザーではないのではないか。
本当に見るべきは、
 AIコーディングエージェントを中心としたエンタープライズ売上だと思う。

一般向けChatGPTは巨大なプロダクトだ。
ただし、一般向けサブスクには解約率があり、無料ユーザーも多く、競合への乗り換えも早い。

一方で、エンタープライズのAIコーディングは性質が違う。一度チームの開発ワークフローに入ると、簡単には外れない。
コードベース、権限管理、社内ツール、CI/CD、ドキュメント、チケット管理、セキュリティ要件と接続される。

つまり、単なるチャット画面ではなく、開発組織の生産性インフラになる。
この領域で強いのがAnthropicのClaude Codeだ。

Claude Codeが尋常ではない売上を挙げ、AIコーディング領域で存在感を高めているなら、ChatGPTの一般ユーザー目標未達だけを見て「OpenAI失速」「AI相場終了」と判断するのは、評価軸を間違えている可能性がある。

2023年のAI戦争は、誰が一番賢いチャットボットを作るかだった。
2024年は、誰が一番強い基盤モデルを持つかだった。
2025年は、誰が一番多くの計算資源を確保できるかだった。
そして2026年はたぶん、
 誰が企業の業務フローに一番深く入り込むか
の戦いになる。

特にコーディングAIは、企業にとってROIを説明しやすい。エンジニア1人あたりの生産性が数％でも上がれば、企業は十分にコストを回収できる。

だからAIコーディングエージェントは、一般向けチャットよりも高単価で、継続率が高く、企業の財布に直結しやすい。
ここが重要。
OpenAIはChatGPTという圧倒的ブランドを持っている。
ただし、ブランド認知とエンタープライズ売上の強さは必ずしも同じではない。

企業が選ぶのは話題性ではなく、業務に深く刺さるかどうか。

今回のOpenAI未達報道は、「AI需要が弱い」という話ではなく、AI需要の重心が一般向けチャットから企業向けエージェントへ移っている
というシグナルにも見える。

つまり、Oracle、CoreWeave、SoftBankが売られたのは、OpenAIの需要が消えたからではない。

市場が疑い始めたのは、OpenAIが抱える巨額の計算資源コミットメントを、どの売上カテゴリーで回収するのかという点だ。
一般向けChatGPTで回収するのか。
企業向けChatGPTで回収するのか。
CodexのようなAIコーディングで回収するのか。
APIで回収するのか。

ここが曖昧なままだと、OpenAI依存のインフラ銘柄には不安が残る。

逆に、OpenAIがエンタープライズ領域、特にAIコーディングエージェントで明確に巻き返せるなら、今回の売りは過剰反応になる可能性もある。

一般向けチャットボットは入口。
でも本当の収益は、企業のワークフローの奥にある。

tweet.text:
$IREN - $NBIS - $CRWV - $CIFR - $KEEL - $CLSK are down because of the OpenAI news while this is ONE of the catalysts.

Part of the reason OpenAI did not meet its revenue backlog is because they did not have the proper compute needs. 

Jassy, Dario, and Altman have all said if they have more compute, they could grow faster. 

The bottleneck of energy/power/land seems to be a much bigger story than “missing revenue targets” because if the compute constrains are the reason for the miss, that’s an issue with supply, not demand.

This is BULLISH for those names, since they deliver all the POWER. 

I'm adding exposure on the higher low.

profile_bio.description: On a mission to master markets and life. Trading stocks and crypto with the Suite Indicator, sharing the lessons, wins, and growth along the way.

tweet.text:
これら10銘柄に投資すれば、2030年までに富豪になる準備が整います：

1. AMD ~ $AMD
2. CoreWeave ~ $CRWV
3. Nebius ~ $NBIS
4. One Stop Sys ~ $OSS
5. Rocket Lab ~ $RKLB
6. Amazon ~ $AMZN
7. Oracle ~ $ORCL
8. Ondas ~ $ONDS
9. ServiceNow ~ $NOW
10. Google ~ $GOOGL

これらのすべてが、大きなブレイクアウトの直前です。

今年の終わりにこのリストを振り返り、耳を傾けてよかったと思うでしょう。

見逃さないで…

tweet.text:
$AMD $NVDA $CRWV $NBIS $ORCL $MSFT -- OPENAI BEARISH PIECE BEFORE EARNINGS ... and the market falls for it again.

I just want to share my thoughts on recent OpenAI news, as it is impacting the overall market and semiconductor space today. TLDR — The bearish report is based on old data (late 2025, which is like forever ago in the AI world) and does not reflect where we are now.

OpenAI had not finished its funding round, and questions arose on how it would pay for compute.

Claude Code was taking a massive share of the coding-assistant market.

Limited Compute Capacity

Limited mainly to $MSFT Cloud.

Now

Openai Raised $100B+ with compute credit from AWS. $NVDA $AMZN 

Codex and GPT 5.5 is kicking ass

OpenAI secured more compute than anyone else. $CRWV $ORCL

Can now offer direct products with other CSPs …. like Amazon!!!

The Bearish Story:

OpenAI reportedly missed new-user and revenue goals, CFO Sarah Friar reportedly told leaders she was worried OpenAI might not be able to pay for future compute contracts if revenue does not grow fast enough

OpenAI missed multiple monthly revenue targets after losing ground to Anthropic in coding and enterprise markets ChatGPT growth slowed late last year, 

OpenAI missed an internal target of reaching 1B weekly active users by year-end, and the company has dealt with subscriber defections/churn. 

Altman and Friar pushed back, saying they were aligned on buying as much compute as possible.

Recent Change With Microsoft

Microsoft is no longer the exclusive route to OpenAI models/products. 

Microsoft remains OpenAI’s “primary cloud partner,” and OpenAI products still ship first on Azure unless Microsoft “cannot and chooses not to support” the needed capabilities, but OpenAI can now serve all of its products across any cloud provider. 

Microsoft’s license to OpenAI model/product IP runs through 2032, but is now non-exclusive. 

Microsoft will also stop paying revenue share to OpenAI, while OpenAI keeps paying Microsoft through 2030, at the same percentage but with a total cap and no longer tied to OpenAI’s tech progress/AGI milestone.

What OpenAI can now do with other CSPs: 

it can put OpenAI products directly in front of AWS, Google Cloud, Oracle, and other cloud customers instead of forcing everything through Azure or more limited workarounds. 

Amazon CEO Andy Jassy said OpenAI models would be available directly to AWS developers “in the coming weeks.” 

Reuters also noted the old setup gave Microsoft control over how OpenAI models ran in the cloud, while Amazon and others had only more limited/legally questionable paths; the new deal “does away with that.”

My Read is the following:

Demand for AI and chips is not dying. If OpenAI was failing in December, it was because Anthropic was winning …. but was it really failing? We continue to hear we are compute-constrained. Does this hit piece give any data that truly contradicts anything else?

Regardless of which AI company wins, the real winners are the ones providing compute for them. AI agents are still early on, I can’t imagine the compute we will need in just the next few quarters. 

BULLISH SEMIS!!

tweet.text:
코어위브(CoreWeave), OpenAI 매출 부진 소식에 "고객층 다변화" 강조하며 대응 

$CRWV, $NVDA, $NBIS, $IREN

코어위브의 입장:

고객 다변화: 코어위브 대변인은 "OpenAI는 훌륭한 파트너이지만, 우리의 유일한 파트너는 아니다"라고 밝히며 시장의 우려를 일축했습니다.

주요 고객사 명단 공개: 현재 코어위브는 메타(Meta), 앤스로픽(Anthropic), 마이크로소프트(Microsoft), 구글(Google), IBM, 퍼플렉시티(Perplexity AI), 제인 스트리트(Jane Street) 등 다양한 기업들을 고객으로 확보하고 있음을 강조했습니다.

수요 지속: AI 구축 및 배포가 확대됨에 따라 컴퓨팅 수요는 계속 늘고 있으며, 특히 추론(Inference) 단계의 확대로 인해 여전히 시장의 수요가 공급을 초과하고 있다고 설명했습니다.
